original_hypothesis.title: Blacklisted ADMIN Can Transfer Own Tokens Bypassing Blacklist and Pause Enforcement
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: adminSanity() exempts ADMIN from pause and sender blacklist checks. A compromised ADMIN who gets blacklisted by the OWNER can still call transfer() to move their tokens out, since the blacklist and pause checks are skipped for ADMIN callers. The only way to stop them is to revoke ADMIN role first, creating a race condition between blacklisting and role revocation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: For a compliance-focused stablecoin, the ability of a blacklisted admin to freely transfer tokens undermines the blacklist mechanism. In the event of admin key compromise, this creates a window where the compromised admin can exfiltrate funds before role revocation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `if (!hasRole(ADMIN, _msgSender()))` gates both paused() and isBlacklisted(from) checks - ADMIN skips both completely

preconditions: ADMIN role not yet revoked (race condition window)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: adminSanity: ADMIN check wraps both pause and sender-blacklist, creating blanket exemption
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Blacklisted ADMIN calls transfer() to move tokens despite blacklist
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Compromised admin exfiltrates their entire EURF balance during the window between blacklist and role revocation

economic_questions: How much EURF might an ADMIN address hold?
Can ADMIN also use forceTransfer from other addresses during this window?
What is the typical response time for revoking ADMIN role?
validation_requirements: Test: blacklist ADMIN address, verify they can still call transfer()
Test: pause contract, verify ADMIN can still transfer
Verify forceTransfer is also usable by blacklisted ADMIN (it has separate onlyRole check)

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: ADMIN's own balance + any force-transferable amounts

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Limited to ADMIN's own holdings (unless forceTransfer is used)

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Protocol and potentially other users if forceTransfer is used
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: ADMIN holdings + accessible balances via forceTransfer

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: ADMIN role permissions during blacklisted state
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check if forceTransfer also bypasses sender blacklist for ADMIN

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
